This is a very common at present, drug targeting via i.n. to brain using nano-
formulation with their help to improve the drugs bioavailability in the brain. So
many challenges still not improved due to BBB is main obstacle including some
other obstacles [50]. We have used a novel developed and optimized P 80 MNG
PLGA  NPs  to  enhance  the  brain  bioavailability  of  Mangiferin  administered  via
i.n. route. This is a first time in history to use opt-P 80 MNG PLGA NPs in the
developed  MCAO  model  of  rats  via  i.n.  route.  P  80  MNG  PLGA  NPs  contains
the  permeation  and  dissolution  enhancer  nature  given  to  treat  MCAO  rats  via
i.n. route. This was seen that MNG staying time was prolonged in nasal mucosa
that means P 80 acts as a strong permeating agent, which increase the higher rate
of  MNG  permeation  followed  by  enhanced  the  bioavailability  of  MNG  in  the
brain  [69].  MNG  was  entrapped  via  P  80  MNG  PLGA  NPs  in  their  core  which
is very helpful to protect the MNG from chemical, extracellular transport by the
P-glycoprotein effluxion, and biological environment. Opt-P 80 MNG PLGA NPs
enhanced  the  bioavailability  of  brain  due  to  stronger  transcellular  transport  of
MNG  by  the  olfactory-neurons  into  the  brain  by  the  use  of  endocytic  pathway
increased significantly via the i.n. administration of P 80 MNG PLGA NPs when it
compared to MNG S (i.n.) and MNG S (i.v.) in the brain due to MNG transported
directly olfactory lobe to brain route bypassing BBB. P 80 MNG PLGA NPs (i.n.)
was  enhanced  MNG  concentration  in  the  brain  for  Cmax  (20.67  times)  &  AUC
0-t (25.25 times) which is also very greater increment in concentration of MNG as
compared to MNG S via i.v. route delivery. Based on our findings, we established
the uptake of MNG to the brain through i.n. has been take placed by two different
pathways.  1.  Pathway,  i.e.,  Systemic:  Drug  was  easily  absorbed  after  that  goes  to
systemic circulation and then uptake by the brain via crossing BBB. 2. Pathway, i.e.,
Olfactory: Drug was partially uptake into brain by the i.n. route. 1. First pathway via
systemic delivery: the absorption of drug and their enrichment to circulate systemi-
cally by the BBB crossing into the brain. 2. Pathway of Olfactory: This way trans-
